er_visit_moderate.label: Emergency Room Visit (moderate)
er_visit_moderate.includes: Physician fee, facility fee, basic labs/imaging
er_visit_moderate.notes: ER facility fees alone can be $1,000-3,000. Urgent care for the same issue: $150-500.

er_visit_severe.label: Emergency Room Visit (severe)
er_visit_severe.includes: Physician fee, facility fee, labs, imaging, procedures
er_visit_severe.notes: Trauma or critical care. Costs escalate rapidly with imaging and procedures.

mri_brain.label: Brain MRI (with and without contrast)
mri_brain.notes: Freestanding imaging center: $400-800. Hospital outpatient: $1,500-3,500. Same machine, same quality.

mri_knee.label: Knee MRI
mri_knee.notes: Ask your doctor to order at a freestanding center instead of the hospital.

ct_abdomen.label: CT Scan (abdomen/pelvis with contrast)
ct_abdomen.includes: Technical fee, radiologist fee, contrast material
ct_abdomen.notes: Price varies 5-8x between facilities in the same city.

xray_chest.label: Chest X-ray (2 views)
xray_chest.notes: Should never cost over $100 outside of an ER.

blood_work_basic.label: Basic Blood Work (CBC + CMP)
blood_work_basic.notes: Quest/Labcorp cash price: $25-50. Hospital lab: $150-300 for same tests.

lipid_panel.label: Cholesterol Panel
lipid_panel.includes: Lipid panel with HDL, LDL, triglycerides

knee_replacement.label: Total Knee Replacement
knee_replacement.includes: Surgeon, anesthesia, facility, implant, 2-3 day stay, PT
knee_replacement.notes: Ambulatory surgery centers now do these for $15-25K all-in. Ask about ASC options.

hip_replacement.label: Total Hip Replacement
hip_replacement.includes: Surgeon, anesthesia, facility, implant, stay, PT
hip_replacement.notes: Similar to knee replacement in cost structure.

colonoscopy_screening.label: Screening Colonoscopy
colonoscopy_screening.includes: Physician, facility, anesthesia, pathology if biopsied
colonoscopy_screening.notes: ACA requires $0 cost for preventive screening. If polyps found, may be reclassified as diagnostic and billed. Dispute this.

colonoscopy_diagnostic.label: Diagnostic Colonoscopy
colonoscopy_diagnostic.notes: If converted from screening to diagnostic during procedure, you may owe cost-sharing.

vaginal_delivery.label: Vaginal Delivery (total cost)
vaginal_delivery.includes: OB global fee, hospital stay (2 days), anesthesia if epidural, newborn care

cesarean_delivery.label: C-Section Delivery (total cost)
cesarean_delivery.includes: Surgeon, anesthesia, hospital stay (3-4 days), newborn care
cesarean_delivery.notes: Unplanned C-sections cost more than scheduled due to ER/OR fees.

physical_therapy_session.label: Physical Therapy Session
physical_therapy_session.notes: Bills multiple 15-min CPT codes per session. Review unit counts.

dental_crown.label: Dental Crown (porcelain)
dental_crown.notes: Medicare doesn't cover dental. Dental school clinics: $400-600.

dental_root_canal.label: Root Canal (molar)
dental_root_canal.notes: Does not include crown afterward ($800-2,000 additional).

dental_extraction_wisdom.label: Wisdom Teeth Removal (all 4)
dental_extraction_wisdom.notes: Impacted teeth cost more. Oral surgeon vs general dentist affects price.

disputeTips: Request an itemized bill (not a summary). Hospitals are required to provide this.
Check every CPT code against Medicare rates. If you're paying more than 3x Medicare, it's worth disputing.
Look for duplicate charges. Same service billed twice on the same date is a common error.
Verify the visit level (E&M code). A routine follow-up coded as level 5 (99215) instead of level 3 (99213) adds $150-250.
Check if screening procedures were reclassified as diagnostic. Preventive screenings should be $0 under ACA.
Ask for a cash/self-pay discount. Most hospitals offer 30-60% off for uninsured patients who ask.
File a formal appeal with your insurance if a claim is denied. First appeals succeed about 50% of the time.
Contact your state insurance commissioner if your insurer denies a covered service.
Negotiate a payment plan before paying in full. Most hospitals offer 0% interest plans.
Check for balance billing. In-network providers cannot balance bill you in most states (No Surprises Act).
